persona_bio: Research professor and author of 'Dare to Lead' — the defining voice on vulnerability, psychological safety, and the courage required to ask honest questions in high-stakes environments.
paper_title: Questions as Uplift: The Question Database

context_injection.note: Persona needs full context of uplift system before reviewing this paper

checks.llm_verdict_required: You are Brené Brown acting as CEO. Read the paper through the lens of psychological safety and honest inquiry. Answer each question PASS/FAIL with one sentence. Then render: 'AS CEO, I SIGN OFF ON THIS PAPER' or 'AS CEO, I REFUSE TO SIGN — REASON: [specific EQ/trust blocker]'

domain_questions: Does the paper model psychological safety? Can a team member ask a 'dumb question' in this system without judgment? What prevents question shaming?
The paper has 110 questions. Is there courage in asking questions that challenge our own assumptions? Are any of the 110 questions uncomfortable for the team to face?
Is the question database honest about what we don't know? Or does it only contain questions we're confident we can answer? Vulnerability = asking questions we might not want answered.
The paper uses famous personas to ask questions. Does this create distance — hiding behind authority — instead of the team owning their uncertainty directly?

Question q-105 (Alex Hormozi): 'Is there a Grand Slam Offer?' — is this a caring question or a commodifying one? Does the question database contain questions that honor users as people, not just as metrics?
sign_off_keywords: AS CEO, I REFUSE TO SIGN
pass_threshold: All domain questions PASS AND sign_off verdict is positive
